questions.explanation: A variable holds a value (e.g., number, string, object) so code can read and update it during execution.

options.A: Storing data values that can be referenced and changed
options.B: Styling HTML elements on a page
options.C: Encrypting network traffic between client and server
options.D: Compiling JavaScript into machine code in the browser

questions.question: In programming, what is a variable primarily used for?

questions.explanation: <main> represents the dominant content of the <body> and should be unique per page.
questions.question: Which HTML5 element is most appropriate for the main content of a document (excluding headers, footers, and sidebars)?

questions.explanation: Media queries apply different CSS rules based on conditions like viewport width, enabling responsive design.
questions.question: Which CSS3 feature is specifically designed to create layouts that adapt to different screen sizes?

questions.explanation: The Elements panel shows the DOM tree and associated styles, allowing live edits for debugging.
questions.question: In browser DevTools, which panel is typically used to inspect and edit the DOM and CSS rules live?

questions.explanation: CSS Grid is designed for two-dimensional layouts, controlling both rows and columns efficiently.
questions.question: You need to create a two-dimensional layout (rows and columns) for a dashboard. Which CSS3 layout system is best suited?

questions.explanation: Icon-only controls need an accessible name (e.g., aria-label or visible text) so assistive tech can announce the purpose.

options.A: Add an accessible name using aria-label (or visible text) describing the action
options.B: Increase the icon size to at least 32px
options.C: Use a <div> instead of a <button> to avoid default button behavior

questions.question: A button is implemented as an icon-only control. Which approach best improves accessibility for screen readers?

questions.explanation: useEffect runs side-effect logic after render and can clean up effects (e.g., unsubscribing) when dependencies change.

options.A: To perform side effects (e.g., data fetching, subscriptions) in function components
options.B: To define global CSS styles for a component
options.C: To create a new component instance on every render
options.D: To replace the need for props by mutating parent state directly

questions.question: In React, what is the primary purpose of the useEffect hook?

questions.explanation: Modern formats and responsive images reduce bytes transferred while keeping the same displayed size and quality targets.

options.A: Serve modern formats (e.g., WebP/AVIF) and use responsive images (srcset/sizes)
options.B: Move all images into a single CSS sprite regardless of size
options.C: Replace tags with background-image for all images
options.D: Disable browser caching to ensure fresh downloads

questions.question: Your page loads slowly due to large images. Which change most directly improves performance without changing visual dimensions?

questions.explanation: A gradual approach (allowJs + staged strictness) minimizes disruption while steadily increasing type safety.

options.A: Enable allowJs, start with strict: false, convert files gradually to .ts/.tsx, then tighten strictness over time
options.B: Rename all .js files to .ts immediately and set noImplicitAny: false permanently
options.C: Avoid type checking by using // @ts-nocheck in every file
options.D: Only add TypeScript types in comments and do not use the TypeScript compiler

questions.question: You are adding TypeScript to an existing JavaScript project. Which approach is generally the safest incremental migration strategy?

questions.explanation: Git provides version control, and platforms like GitHub/GitLab add workflows for reviews, branching, tags, and releases.

options.A: Git (with a hosted platform like GitHub/GitLab for PRs and releases)

questions.question: A team needs to track changes, review code, and manage releases for a web app. Which tool best fits software configuration management needs?
